signal_points: Miners are facing critical margin compression with hashprice below breakeven for mid-tier machines.
STRC trading at par suggests market pricing in liquidation value, not premium yield demand.
A significant difficulty adjustment is imminent, which will reveal true miner capitulation.

btc_price: BTC price decline is pressuring miner margins, exacerbating the post-halving hashprice compression.
Any sustained BTC price dip below $60k will accelerate miner capitulation and hash rate decline.
hashprice: Current hashprice of $31.2/PH/day is below S19 XP breakeven, indicating significant stress for many miners.
Hashprice is driven by BTC price weakness and increasing network hashrate, compounding profitability issues.
difficulty: Difficulty is stable, but failing to adjust downwards means lower-tier miners face immediate profitability risk.
Hash rate growth has stalled; a significant difficulty increase is unlikely unless BTC price rebounds sharply.
bmn_revenue: BMN daily revenue is under pressure due to low hashprice, despite stable fleet uptime.
BMN yield will likely decline further if hashprice remains depressed and fleet allocation is unchanged.
strc: STRC trading at par ($99.97) signals the market values it at liquidation preference, not yield.
A stable BTC Rating (4.1x) implies no significant BTC sales or dilution, but also no accretive purchases.
bmn_hashrate: BMN fleet shows high uptime and operational efficiency, securing its share of rewards.
BMN's 27.8 EH/s is a stable portion of the network, but low hashprice limits its revenue generation.
